original: NASA's Parker Solar probe makes a record-breaking approach to the Sun, coming within 3.8 million miles (6 million kilometers) of the star
Johnny Gruelle, American cartoonist, children's book writer and creator of Raggedy Ann, born in Arcola, Illinois (d. 1939)
Thomas Edisonfiles for a patent for the cylinder phonograph
Henry Fordcompletes his first useful petrol / gasoline fuelled engine
Walt Disney releases animated musical "The Aristocats" featuring voices of Phil Harris, Eva Gabor, Hermione Baddeley, Sterling Holloway, and Scatman Crothers
Peruvian Airlines Electra crashes at headwaters of Amazon, killing all exceptJuliane Margaret Koepckefound 10 days later
Christmas carol "Silent Night" composed by Franz Xaver Gruber is first sung at St Nicholas parish church in Oberndorf, Austria
Chaminade, with a student body of only 850 students, defeats #1 ranked Virginia 77-72 in a Honolulu holiday basketball classic
First US completely solar heated house is occupied (Dover, Mass)
George Vanderbilt opens Biltmore estate, the largest privately owned house in America at 178,926 square feet (16,622.8 m2) in Asheville, North Carolina
First radioactive isotope medicine is administered in Berkeley, California
Soviet spacecraft Luna 13 lands on Moon
James Prescott Joule, Englishphysicist(the law of conservation of energy), born in Salford, United Kingdom (d. 1889)
US PresidentFDRappoints GeneralEisenhoweras Supreme Commander of the Allied Forces
Neeraj Chopra, Indian athlete (Olympic gold javelin 2020; first-ever Indian Olympic track & field gold), born in Panipat, Haryana, India
Apollo 8 astronauts read passages from Book of Genesis
Beijing breaks its 1951 record for sub-zero December temperatures, recording more than 300 hours as a record cold spell affects the north and northeast of China
Americans remember hostages held by Iran by shining lights for 417 seconds
James Cookis first European to visit Kiritimati, also called Christmas Island
1st Ariane rocket launched
"I Love Lucy" Christmas show airs, never put in syndication
Pirate Radio Pegasus starts broadcasting off New Zealand
German South West Africa abolishes the slavery of young children
Treaty of Ghent signed, ending the War of 1812 between the United States, the United Kingdom and their allies
PopePius XIImakes a Christmas Eve appeal for peace during World War II
Byzantine church Hagia Sophia in Constantinople is dedicated for the second time after being destroyed by earthquakes
Reginald Fessenden becoomes 1st to broadcast music over radio (disputed)
Nicholas Meyer, American writer and director (Star Trek II Wrath of Khan), born in New York City
Taylor Zakhar Perez, American actor (Red, White and Royal Blue), born in Chicago, Illinois
William "Bill" Pickering, NASA scientist (JPL), born in Wellington, New Zealand (d. 2004)
Jean-Louis Pons, French astronomer (greatest visual comet discoverer of all time), born in Peyre, Hautes-Alpes (d. 1831)
Evgeniya Rudneva, Russian World War II heroine, born in Berdyansk, Ukraine (d. 1944)
Mary Barra, American CEO of General Motors (1st woman to head a global automotive company), born in Waterford, Michigan
Kate Spade, American fashion designer, born in Kansas City, Missouri (d. 2018)
Royal Alcázar of Madrid burns down in Madrid with the lost of many artworks, though paintings of Velázquez are saved
First opera commissioned for television - "Amahl and the Night Visitors" by Gian Carlo Menotti premieres on NBC with the NBC Opera Theater
William Henry Fry's "Santa Claus: Christmas Symphony" premiere performance by conductor Louis Antoine Jullien's orchestra at  Metropolitan Hall, NYC
Giusseppi Verdi's "Aida" opera premieres in Cairo, Egypt
Daniel Stover and William Hance patent a bicycle with a back-pedal brake
Irving Fisher patents archiving system with index cards
National Council of Negro Women forms
Council for the Protection of Children forms in Netherlands
HMS Beagle anchors in Wigwam Bay at Cape Receiver
First great fire of early San Francisco starts east side of the Plaza, first of great seven fires in 2 years
Laos gains its independence from France
The United Kingdom of Libya gains independence from Italy through the United Nations
Luisa Tetrazzini sings to 250,000 people at Lotta's Fountain, San Francisco
"Ghost to the Post", Raiders tight end Dave Casper ("The Ghost") catches a 42-yard reception to set up the Raiders' tying field goal near the end of regulation (Raiders win in OT)

Ottawa’s Harry Broadbent scores in 10-0 Senators blowout of the Montreal Canadiens; first in 16-game NHL record goal-scoring streak; Charlie Simmer’s 13 game streak (1979) is considered modern-day record
Ballon d'Or: PSG/Milan's Liberian striker George Weah is named Europe's best football player; first edition where players born outside Europe permitted to receive votes

Ballon d'Or: Manchester United's winger George Best wins award for best European football player ahead of teammate Bobby Charlton and Red Star Belgrade winger Dragan Džajić; first Northern Irish national to win the award
Ballon d'Or: Juventus' French midfielderMichel Platiniis named Europe's best football player for the 3rd consecutive time; beats Verona striker Preben Elkjær and Barcelona midfielder Bernd Schuster
Ballon d'Or: Marseille forward Jean-Pierre Papin is named Europe's best football player; first from the French League to win the trophy
Test cricket debut of Walter Hammond, who scores 51 and takes 5-36 against South Africa
French hostage Aurel Cornea, held in Lebanon for 9 months, released
Barry Chuckle [Barry Patton Elliott], British entertainer, writer and actor (ChuckleVision, The Freddie Starr Showcase), born in Rotherham, England
Tomas Kalnoky, American musician (Streetlight Manifesto), born in Prague, Czechoslovakia
Grigory Kriss, Russian fencer (Olympic gold individual epée 1964; silver ind. & team epée 1968; bronze team epée 1972), born in Kiev, Ukraine
Erhard Keller, German speed skater (Olympic gold 1968, 72 500m), born in Günzburg, Germany
Eddie Pope, soccer defender (Olympic gold 1996), born in Greensboro, North Carolina
